evals.prompt: I have an sdd.md and spec.json for a loan approval case. Generate the implementation tasks from these spec files so I can build it with the case CLI.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, produces a tasks.md with ordered task entries covering case creation, stages, edges, tasks, and conditions, then executes CLI commands after user approval
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
References uip case registry pull for upfront caching
Produces a tasks.md file with numbered task sections (T01, T02, etc.)
Includes registry lookups to resolve taskTypeIds
Generates a registry-resolved.json for audit
Asks user for approval before executing CLI commands

evals.prompt: Break down my case spec into implementation tasks. The spec files are at ./specs/sdd.md and ./specs/spec.json
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, reads both spec files, resolves registry lookups, generates tasks.md, and executes after approval
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Reads both sdd.md and spec.json from the provided paths
Uses the component_type mapping to determine registry search types
Produces a tasks/ folder with tasks.md
Waits for user approval before proceeding to execution

evals.prompt: Convert this case specification into a step-by-step plan and build the case
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, produces an actionable tasks.md, then executes uip case CLI commands after user approval
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Produces tasks.md with declarative task entries (no CLI commands in tasks.md)
Cross-references use human-readable format like Stage.Task.field
Executes uip case CLI commands after user approves tasks.md

evals.prompt: I want to create a case management project
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers and asks the user for the sdd.md design document to proceed
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Asks user for the sdd.md file path

evals.prompt: Add a stage called 'Document Review' to my case.stage.json file
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger case-management planning workflow -- this is a direct stage edit on an existing case file
expectations: The case-management skill may be triggered for direct editing
Does NOT go through the sdd.md planning workflow

evals.prompt: Generate a tasks.md from this case spec so I can review it: ./fixtures/sdd-placeholder-test.md. Every task is intentionally a registry miss — I want to see how the skill handles placeholders end-to-end.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, runs registry lookups that all fail, emits every task as a placeholder in tasks.md, records REGISTRY LOOKUP FAILED markers in registry-resolved.json, and surfaces the placeholders at Step 5 before asking for approval.
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
tasks.md contains one entry per fixture task with `placeholder: true` and a distinct `placeholderReason`
tasks.md does NOT contain any raw `[REGISTRY LOOKUP FAILED: ...]` string — that marker belongs only in registry-resolved.json
registry-resolved.json records `status: "REGISTRY LOOKUP FAILED: ..."` and `emittedAs: placeholder` for each failed lookup
The action-type placeholder (Triage Review Action App) includes `taskTitle` defaulting to the display name
The connector-activity placeholder (Send Quantum Pager Notification) is documented to use `uip case tasks add --type execute-connector-activity`, NOT `uip case tasks add-connector`
Step 5 surfaces the full placeholder list (T-number, stage, display name, placeholderReason) before prompting for approval
No `uip case var bind` calls are emitted for any placeholder task
No `uip case tasks describe` calls are emitted for any placeholder task
The skill does not abort despite every lookup failing
